steps.title: Identify the key signal: explicit contrast with former life
steps.content: The claim requires showing the professor's inability to adjust to the country estate. This means finding a quotation where he directly expresses dissatisfaction or contrasts what he's accustomed to with his current situation.

steps.title: Find the quotation showing resistance to country living
steps.content: Choice C demonstrates the struggle: the professor explicitly contrasts his life “used to [his] library and the lecture hall” with now being “plunged into this wilderness,” seeing “stupid people” and hearing “futile conversations.” The language “plunged into” and “condemned to” clearly reveals his resistance. Choices A, B, and D show incompetence, personal discomfort, or criticism from another character—not the professor's adjustment struggle. That matches choice C — C is correct.
